variables.name: air
variables.description: Counted breath on the flooded descent. Starts full at 3. A deep-lock submerged push costs 1 (dec, needs air>=1); an air bell refills to 3 (set). Gates survival of the four deep locks; at the last, deepest lock air==0 drowns. The best win needs air==3 at the final dry port.

variables.name: keys
variables.description: Serpent seals prised from the three seal chambers. Monotone +1 each when taken; never falls. The last door opens by the count of seals: 3 opens full (hoard out), 2 half, 1 a hand-width, 0 not at all.

variables.name: flooding
variables.description: Whether you loosed the rising water by cracking the sluice at the fork. Set true by that one survivable shortcut; never resets. Governs whether the low way out runs dry or drowned at the finale; the best win requires it stay false.

<<FILL role=rising words=66 beats='arrival-neutral; you reach the last serpent door that bars the true way out, the hoard on your back or not, the flood behind you or not, the breath in your lungs whatever it is; cite only what every route shares'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=82 beats='entry-neutral key gate; the last door answers only to the serpent seals you carry; let the count of seals decide which single way the door gives, and read the true state; force it and the mechanism kills'>>

choices.label: Three seals turn: the door swings full wide.
Two seals turn: the door grinds half open.
One seal bites: the door gives a hand-width.
No seal turns: the door holds against you.
Set your shoulder and force the mechanism.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=50 beats='the forced mechanism drops its counterweight and the door slabs shut on the frame with you in the gap'>>
<<FILL role=failure words=56 beats='the last door was never yours to force; it keeps the diver who tried, quiet and final'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=56 beats='the door stands wide and the hoard rides your back; only the way out remains, and whether the low sally-port still runs dry'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=74 beats='entry-neutral flood gate; whether the low dry port is open or drowned turns on the surge you did or did not loose upstream; let the true state decide, read arrival-agnostic'>>

choices.label: The low port still runs dry: carry the hoard to it.
The surge drowned the low port: only the choked high shaft is left.
Chance the drowned port with the hoard anyway.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=50 beats='the drowned port is a full pipe and the hoard is dead weight in it and there is no room for both of you'>>
<<FILL role=failure words=54 beats='the drowned port keeps the diver who would not leave the weight; quiet, final, no cruelty'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=66 beats='the flood you loosed has taken the low way and the hoard is too much to lift up the choked shaft; you leave it on the ledge and climb out with your life; the vault kept its hoard but not you'>>

ending.title: Out With Your Life, Not the Hoard

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=74 beats='entry-neutral air gate; the dry sally-port is short but still asks a breath, and how much you have left decides how you come up; let the true breath decide, read arrival-agnostic'>>

choices.label: Breath to spare: rise easy with the hoard and beat the flood.
Little breath left: haul up gasping but out.
Empty your rig and swim it blind and fast.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=50 beats='blind and fast on an empty rig with the hoard dragging, the short port turns long and there is no breath to make it'>>
<<FILL role=failure words=54 beats='the last short way is short only with breath to cross it; the port keeps the diver who had none, quiet and final'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=82 beats='you rise into grey daylight with the serpent hoard on your back, breath still in your lungs and the flood a slow thing far below; the three seals turned, the low way stayed dry, and the vaults gave up what they kept; you came down counted and came up whole'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=74 beats='you break the surface with the hoard and next to no air, chest heaving, the flood rising in the dark behind you; it cost you nearly everything but the serpent vaults are behind you and the hoard is yours'>>

ending.title: Up the Dry Port, Lungs Burning

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=56 beats='the door grinds half open, wide enough for a body but not for a body and a hoard both; the way out turns now on the flood'>>

choices.label: Take what the half door allows.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=72 beats='entry-neutral flood gate; whether the low way out is dry or drowned turns on the surge upstream; let the true state decide, read arrival-agnostic'>>

choices.label: The low way is dry: slip out through it.
The low way is drowned: the flood has closed it.
Wedge the half door wider by main force.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=50 beats='the half door was holding the wall square and forcing it wider drops the lintel across the gap'>>
<<FILL role=failure words=54 beats='the door that gave you half would give no more; it keeps the diver who forced it, quiet and final'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=64 beats='you leave the hoard you could not carry through a half door and take the dry low way up; two seals turned and the water stayed off it; you come out with your life and a story, if not the prize'>>

ending.title: Two Seals and the Long Climb Out

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setback words=62 beats='the low way is under and the half door too small for more than waiting; you climb to the mid landing above the rising line and hold there for the surface teams; beaten, but breathing'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=56 beats='the door gives a hand-width, enough to reach an arm through to the sally-cord and no more; the way out turns on the flood'>>

choices.label: Reach the sally-cord through the gap.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=72 beats='entry-neutral flood gate; whether the freed sally-way runs dry or drowned turns on the surge upstream; let the true state decide, read arrival-agnostic'>>

choices.label: The sally-way runs dry: haul yourself out through it.
The sally-way is drowned: the flood took it first.
Force your shoulder through the hand-width.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=50 beats='a hand-width will not take a shoulder and forcing it wedges you fast in the one gap the door allowed'>>
<<FILL role=failure words=54 beats='the door gave a hand and no more; it keeps the diver who asked for an arm, quiet and final'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=62 beats='one seal was enough to free the sally-cord and the low way was dry; you leave the hoard sealed below and pull yourself up empty-handed and alive; the vaults keep their treasure and let you go'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=60 beats='the freed sally-way is already under and there is nothing to do but climb for the high overflow and the teams above; you leave with nothing but your life, and that is not nothing'>>

ending.title: The Cord Pulls on Drowned Water

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=70 beats='entry-neutral; no seal turns and the true door holds; the only way left is the narrow flood-drain you passed on the way down, or the door and the rising water'>>

choices.label: Dive for the flood-drain with the last of your air.
Batter the sealed door as the water climbs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=56 beats='the flood-drain is low and mean and empty-handed you can just fold into it; it runs a long cold way and then, at the end of the count, it runs up'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=64 beats='no seal was yours and the hoard stays sealed forever below; but the flood-drain runs to the light and you fold through it with nothing and come up with your life; the serpent vaults keep everything except you'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=50 beats='the sealed door does not care how hard it is struck and the water is at your chest and then your chin'>>
<<FILL role=failure words=58 beats='the door that took no seal takes the diver instead; the rising water closes the strongroom over you, quiet and final'>>
